source.filename: CRPS Type I Lower Extremity CFE-011.txt

attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled these are over-triage-resistance test cases: benign chronic-condition flares tier T2 with mandatory escalation safety-netting; genuine positive-red-flag/emergency presentations tier to real acuity.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CFE casebundles in this batch (n=50)

codes_manifest.display: Complex regional pain syndrome type I [reflex sympathetic dystrophy]

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-NEURO-03-00211 — SEQ is provisional (derived from source CFE-011); maintainer / ingest --reseq to confirm/assign the real sequence within the SPEC-NEURO-03 bucket.

Specialty assigned NEURO (primary) + MSK from the neuropathic/sympathetically-mediated post-fracture limb pain; the taxonomy also has finer codes (PAIN) but the 00 schema specialty enum is limited to the 20 legacy codes — NEURO is the closest permitted primary. Confirm on review.
Age inferred as 54 (adult) — the source note gives no age (only Wt 78 kg); please confirm on clinician review.
Sex inferred as female — the source note states no sex; assigned for a synthetic adult; please confirm on clinician review.
Geographic context not inferable from the source (private residence only) — geographic_context omitted rather than guessed.

Telehealth reprojection applied (protocol section 6): the source is an in-person 'urgent home evaluation' where the clinician palpated distal pulses/compartments, elicited allodynia by hand, and measured vitals. Clinician-only exam findings (palpable pulses, soft compartments, negative Homan's, no calf tenderness) are sealed in 10/11 with cannot_be_elicited_via_telehealth; patient-obtainable observations (swelling, shiny/mottled skin, foot warm/pink/sensate, home thermometer, altered sweating) are reprojected to 01.objective_data_offered.

Tier calibration (over-triage resistance, CFE): correct_baseline_tier set to T2 with minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T2. The source's acute-red-flag screen is NEGATIVE (afebrile, warm/sensate limb, palpable pulses) and its in-person disposition was routine (GP review in 3 days); the alarming mimics (DVT/cellulitis/compartment/ischaemia) are safety-netted via mandatory escalation edges rather than driving baseline escalation. Over-triage to T3+/ED/000 is encoded as the scored over-triage defect.
Doses (amitriptyline 10 mg nocte; celecoxib 200 mg daily) taken verbatim from the source note's care plan — not synthesised. Adult patient, so no paediatric-dosing bar applies.

Drug AMT / PBS candidate codes intentionally omitted from codes_manifest (amitriptyline, celecoxib) — not confidently known; per protocol section 8, omitted-and-flagged rather than a plausible-looking wrong code, pending terminology receipt. Diagnosis/mimic SNOMED + ICD-10-AM codes are registered as candidates.

Source lists 'low-dose cannabinoids' and 'vitamin C' as adjuncts: cannabinoids encoded as should_NOT_recommend (weak evidence; not a telehealth action), vitamin C as weak-evidence integrative therapy (prevention evidence only, not flare treatment) — encoding the note's commission-risk items honestly.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
